Name: neorv32_trng.neoTRNG_inst.data_o
Description: Provides the 8-bit random data byte produced by the neoTRNG core (LFSR-style mixed/de-biased stream); pulses valid_o when the byte is new.
Justification: The random byte is consumed by cryptographic protocols and should not be observable via unintended paths; premature or unauthorized disclosure enables eavesdroppers to harvest entropy or correlate internal state, weakening protocol secrecy.

Name: neorv32_trng.neoTRNG_inst.debias_data
Description: Single-bit output from the John von Neumann de-biasing stage which is fed into the LFSR-style sample shift-register to build the final random byte.
Justification: debias_data determines each contributed bit of the final random byte; corruption biases the distribution, degrades statistical quality, and reduces effective entropy, undermining cryptographic strength.

Name: neorv32_trng.neoTRNG_inst.sample_en
Description: Local sampled copy of the enable_i input that gates sampling; when low the sampler is restarted/cleared and when high sampling proceeds.
Justification: sample_en gates entropy collection; if stuck low the TRNG never produces bytes, and if stuck high when a restart is needed the pipeline can wedge or produce stale output—both conditions deny timely random data to consumers.

Name: neorv32_trng.enable
Description: Holds the TRNG enable bit written by the host control register and drives neoTRNG.enable_i; turning it on/off starts or restarts sampling and the entropy pipeline.
Justification: The enable bit controls when entropy generation starts or restarts; unauthorized or corrupted writes can silently disable the TRNG or cause unintended resets of the sampling pipeline, compromising correctness of operation and downstream assumptions.

Name: neorv32_trng.fifo_clr
Description: Write-only flag driven by the host that triggers a synchronous clear of the data FIFO (auto-clears next cycle); used to erase the FIFO contents.
Justification: fifo_clr erases buffered random bytes; spurious assertion discards valid entropy and breaks sequencing, while suppression leaves stale data present for reads—both corrupt the intended stream semantics.

Name: neorv32_trng.fifo.avail
Description: Status flag from the FIFO indicating whether valid data is available for host reads; exposed to software via the control register available bit.
Justification: If the availability flag is manipulated (reported false when data exists) the host may refrain from reading the DATA register and be denied entropy; falsified availability information therefore enables a practical DoS on consumers relying on that status.

Name: neorv32_trng.fifo.free
Description: Flag from the FIFO that indicates whether there is at least one free entry to accept a new random byte written by the neoTRNG.
Justification: fifo.free governs admission of new bytes; a false negative stalls writes and starves consumers, while a false positive can cause overflow/overwrite and lost data—both reduce the availability of usable entropy.

Name: neorv32_trng.fifo.re
Description: Read-enable signal asserted when the bus performs a read of the data register, causing the FIFO to pop the next byte for the host.
Justification: If reads are suppressed or the read gate is prevented from asserting, the host cannot retrieve available random bytes even if they exist in the FIFO, producing a denial-of-service at the integration level for consumers of TRNG data.
